items.html: Let us clarify the discernment of the wine, which is one of them — why it requires a seal within a seal, and what the matter of these two seals is. The matter: know that the wine is the discernments of the five Gevurot [judgments] in the Yesod of the Nukva [female], as is known; they themselves are the Mayin Nukvin [female waters] in her, and they are called MaNTzePaCh. And it is known that all the outer ones and the Klipot [shells] draw all their vitality from the dregs of the upper wine. They stand beneath the Nukva, as is known, in the secret of "her feet go down to death," and they grasp only at her ending and beneath her. While these Gevurot, called wine, are guarded within the Yesod of the female with two seals, a seal upon a seal, they are called "the wine that gladdens," which is the discernment of the Gevurot when they are sweetened. But at times those Gevurot turn over from the gladdening wine into blood, which is complete judgments, and then menstrual blood [Dam Nidot] is made; and when it goes outside, the outer ones suckle this menstrual blood from them. Below it will be clarified that the Gevurot themselves become the blood, and the seal is called Nidat — and this is "the blood of Nidat." Understand this.
